pubmed_links: [Shioda et al. (2017) Rabex-5/PDGFRβ signaling links dopamine D2R endocytosis to dendritic spine formation in striatopallidal neurons](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27922607/); [Mori et al. (2013) Rabex-5 regulates dendritic localization of Rab17 and neurite morphogenesis in hippocampal neurons](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23430262/); [Wu et al. (2014) Semaphorin 3A activates Rab5 via Rabex-5 to promote growth cone collapse and organize callosal axon projections](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25161316/)
summary: RABGEF1 encodes Rabex-5, a guanine nucleotide exchange factor for Rab5 that regulates endosomal trafficking in neurons, where it mediates dopamine D2 receptor endocytosis and downstream ERK/PDGFRβ signaling required for dendritic spine formation in striatopallidal neurons and is implicated in antipsychotic drug mechanisms. It also controls dendritic morphogenesis in hippocampal neurons and semaphorin-guided callosal axon guidance, placing it in key neurodevelopmental and dopaminergic pathways relevant to psychiatric disorders. (emerging evidence)
